methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Navier is a seed-stage transportation company that has raised $7.2M to date. The transportation sector peer universe consists of seven private companies including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft, with no listed public comparables available.

A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient narrow-filter peers meeting stage and valuation-tier criteria—only zero same-stage and one adjacent-stage transportation company were identified, falling short of the three-datapoint minimum for reliable triangulation. Stage-progression methodology is applicable and forms the basis of this analysis. Using the $7.2M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier yields an implied valuation of $18M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $15M to $21M, with $18M as the median point. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty. The primary limitation is the sparse peer set in transportation at seed stage, which prevents cross-validation through comparable company multiples. The valuation depends substantially on the reasonableness of the 2.5x step-up assumption for this specific company and sector. Resolution of key commercialization risks—including regulatory pathway clarity, manufacturing feasibility, and market traction—would materially inform a narrower range in subsequent rounds.
